_comment: GENERATED by scripts/gen-tools.mjs — do not edit by hand. Derives from apache/magpie tools/, spec-loop specs, skills/, docs/, and organizations/.

capabilities.triage: Sweep a queue, classify candidates, propose dispositions for human confirmation.
capabilities.review: Deep per-item code review of a PR or local diff; also contributor mentoring (single-item teaching intervention).
capabilities.fix: Implement a code change against an upstream repo to resolve a triaged issue.
capabilities.intake: Import external signal (mailing list, scan report, public PR) into a tracker entry, or keep an existing entry reconciled with one of those sources.
capabilities.reconciliation: Compare tracker state against an external inventory (e.g. ASF security dashboard, organization-wide issue registry); surface drift; propose corrections. Does **not** write to either source.
capabilities.resolve: Close-out actions: invalidate, dedupe, CVE-allocate, post-announcement housekeeping.
capabilities.reassess: Re-run resolved or end-of-life issues against current code to verify still-fixed / still-broken.
capabilities.stats: Read-only dashboards, metrics, governance evidence, contributor nomination briefs.
capabilities.platform: Framework / agent substrate skills: install, verify, update, doctor, override-upstream, status, shared-config-sync, the `setup` bootstrap.
capabilities.authoring: Skills that author or maintain other skills: `write-skill`, `optimize-skill`.

substrates.sandbox: Agent isolation, egress control, settings audit.
substrates.framework-dev: Build / validate / eval the framework itself.

tools.name: agent-guard
tools.title: agent-guard
tools.description: A deterministic Claude Code PreToolUse guard dispatcher. It inspects every Bash command before it runs and must not depend on the model remembering a SKILL.md instruction.

tools.name: agent-isolation
tools.title: agent-isolation
tools.description: This directory ships the moving pieces the framework's docs/setup/secure-agent-setup.md document references. It is not a Python project (unlike the sibling tools under tools/cve-tool-vulnogram/ and tools/gmail/oauth-draft/) — these are…

tools.name: apache-projects
tools.title: apache-projects
tools.description: ASF project-metadata substrate. Read-only, unauthenticated client for the official apache/comdev apache-projects-mcp server, which wraps the public projects.apache.org/json feeds (committee / committer rosters, people + Apache IDs,…

tools.name: cve-org
tools.title: cve-org
tools.description: CVE.org publication client. Submits CVE records via the CVE.org REST API; consumed by security-cve-allocate once a CVE has been allocated via the ASF Vulnogram path. See tool.md for the protocol detail and cve.org field mapping.

tools.name: cve-tool
tools.title: cve-tool
tools.description: The framework's CVE-tooling adapter contract. Today every CVE-aware skill in this repository — security-cve-allocate, security-issue-sync, security-issue-invalidate, security-issue-deduplicate — speaks Vulnogram. They embed Vulnogram's…

tools.name: cve-tool-vulnogram
tools.title: cve-tool-vulnogram
tools.description: ASF Vulnogram CVE-allocation client. OAuth-authenticated API that allocates a CVE ID through the ASF's Vulnogram instance and publishes the CVE record. Consumed by security-cve-allocate. See allocation.md, record.md, and…

tools.name: dashboard-generator
tools.title: Dashboard generator
tools.description: Deterministic reference implementations of the dashboard that issue-reassess-stats produces. Adopters who want CI-rendered dashboards (refreshed on schedule, published as a build artefact) use one of these reference scripts instead of…

tools.name: dev
tools.title: dev
tools.description: Framework dev-loop helpers (placeholder check, agent pre-commit hook). Invoked by prek and CI; not consumed by any skill directly. See the individual scripts in this directory for usage.

tools.name: egress-gateway
tools.title: egress-gateway
tools.description: A local host-allowlisting HTTP(S) forward proxy for the Magpie framework. It is the egress-control chokepoint: framework tools point HTTPS_PROXY/HTTP_PROXY at it, and the gateway rejects any connection to a host that is not on its…

tools.name: forwarder-relay
tools.title: forwarder-relay
tools.description: A forwarder-relay adapter is a pluggable seam that teaches the security skills how to recognise an inbound report that arrived reporter's message to the project), how to extract the original-reporter credit from the relayed body, and…

tools.name: github
tools.title: github
tools.description: GitHub REST + GraphQL substrate. Pure read/write wrapper used by every lifecycle phase (triage / intake / fix / resolve / stats). See tool.md for the operation catalogue and the per-area files (issue-template.md, labels.md,…

tools.name: github-body-field
tools.title: github-body-field
tools.description: Read or rewrite a single ### Field section of a GitHub issue body without bringing the body into agent context.

tools.name: github-rollup
tools.title: github-rollup
tools.description: Append to (or create) the status-rollup comment on a GitHub issue without bringing the rollup body into agent context.

tools.name: gmail
tools.title: gmail
tools.description: Gmail API substrate. Read + draft-only — never sends. Provides two contracts: mail-source for inbound report intake (search / read a uniform thread/message view) and mail-draft for outbound courtesy-reply drafting. Used by the…

tools.name: jira
tools.title: JIRA bridge
tools.description: JIRA REST helpers for the issue-* skill family. Adopters with JIRA-based issue trackers wire this in as their tracker bridge; adopters using GitHub Issues or other trackers contribute a parallel tools/<tracker>/ directory.

tools.name: mail-archive
tools.title: mail-archive
tools.description: This file defines the adapter contract for public mail-archive backends — the seam that lets adopting projects plug a non-ASF archive system (Hyperkitty, Discourse, Google Groups, GitHub Discussions, or none at all) into the same skills…

tools.name: mail-source
tools.title: mail-source
tools.description: Mail-source backend abstraction. Pluggable backends (mbox, IMAP, the Gmail API via tools/gmail, future Mailman 3 / Hyperkitty) that feed the security-issue-import intake pipeline a uniform thread/message view. See contract.md for the…

tools.name: permission-audit
tools.title: permission-audit
tools.description: Audit + atomically edit Claude Code's permissions.allow[] entries in <repo>/.claude/settings.json and <repo>/.claude/settings.local.json.

tools.name: ponymail
tools.title: ponymail
tools.description: PonyMail archive substrate. Read-only ASF mailing-list archive client; complements gmail for threads not present in the inbox. Used by security-issue-import + sync to cross-reference public mailing-list discussions. See tool.md for the…

tools.name: pr-management-stats
tools.title: pr-management-stats reference implementation
tools.description: Deterministic reference implementation of the data-fetch + classification contract that backs the pr-management-stats skill.

tools.name: preflight-audit
tools.title: preflight-audit
tools.description: Dry-run the bulk-mode pre-flight classifier against a real or replayed tracker. Use to measure skip-rate before / after any rule change — closes the tune-then-verify loop so rule edits are made against evidence, not guesswork.

tools.name: privacy-llm
tools.title: privacy-llm
tools.description: Privacy-LLM PII-scrubbing gate. Standalone redactor / checker pair that screens content for PII before it reaches an external LLM. See tool.md and wiring.md for integration details, models.md for the model catalogue, and pii.md for the…

tools.name: probe-templates
tools.title: Probe templates
tools.description: Runnable cross-family probe scripts that the issue-reproducer skill copies from when its Step 9 (optional cross-family probe) runs against an issue.

tools.name: sandbox-lint
tools.title: sandbox-lint
tools.description: Lints .claude/settings.json against the shipped baseline at tools/sandbox-lint/expected.json, and against the security invariants documented in docs/security/threat-model.md (mitigation M.29). The threat-model document lands in a…

tools.name: scan-format
tools.title: scan-format
tools.description: A scan-format adapter teaches security-issue-import-from-scan how to read one security scanner's report layout. The skill is scanner-agnostic; everything format-specific — how to recognise a scan folder, how to enumerate findings, how…

tools.name: security-tracker-stats-dashboard
tools.title: security-tracker-stats-dashboard
tools.description: Generate a self-contained HTML dashboard of <tracker> repository statistics — issue-lifecycle bands (untriaged / triaged / PR-merged / fixed-released / closed-other), opened-vs-untriaged backlog, cumulative opened/closed, and…

tools.name: skill-and-tool-validator
tools.title: skill-and-tool-validator
tools.description: Validate framework skill definitions — YAML frontmatter, internal link integrity, and placeholder conventions.

tools.name: skill-evals
tools.title: skill-evals
tools.description: Behavioral eval harness for Apache Magpie skills. Each eval suite tests a skill pipeline step by step, verifying that the model produces the correct structured JSON output for a fixed set of fixture cases.

tools.name: spec-loop
tools.title: spec-loop
tools.description: A spec-driven build loop for this framework, in the general Ralph style (run a fresh agent context against a fixed prompt, repeat), adapted to the framework's human-in-the-loop posture. The full write-up is in…

tools.name: spec-status-index
tools.title: spec-status-index
tools.description: A deterministic uv tool that reads spec-loop specs from tools/spec-loop/specs/ and prints them grouped by status, so build iterations can choose the next work item mechanically.

tools.name: spec-validator
tools.title: spec-validator
tools.description: Validates spec files in tools/spec-loop/specs/ — the counterpart to tools/skill-and-tool-validator/ for the spec side of the framework.

tools.name: vcs
tools.title: magpie-vcs
tools.description: Runnable implementation of the source-control (VCS) capability documented in tools/github/source-control.md. It extracts the abstraction the dev-loop skills assume — branch, stage, commit, diff, log, fetch, push, working-tree reset —…
